---
title: "Multiple label layouts + `;` to cycle them"
author: "Yun Jiang"
date: "2026-07-23"
format:
  html:
    html-math-method: mathjax
include-in-header:
  text: |
    <link rel="icon" href="data:image/svg+xml,<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100'><text y='.9em' font-size='90'>🎛️</text></svg>">
---

# Objective

Give the overlay more than one grid geometry (preset) and let the user flip between
them **live, while the overlay is up**, with a single key — e.g. a dense edge grid
versus a uniform full-screen grid — remembering the last choice across Esc/reopen and
a full restart.

# Why it fits the existing architecture

The grid engine is already fully parameterized by five App.config knobs, read in
`EnumGridHints` (`src/HuntAndPeck/Services/UiAutomationHintProviderService.cs`):
`GridEdgeStep`, `GridCenterStep`, `GridInset`, `GridEdgeBandPercent`,
`GridDenseRegions`. A "layout" is just a named tuple of those five. The requested
"uniform step" layout already works: `GridDenseRegions=Center` alone fills the whole
window at one step, so no new geometry code is needed — only preset storage, live
cycling, and persistence.

Three existing mechanisms are reused rather than reinvented:

1. **`CycleMonitor` (Tab)** already swaps the session list and re-renders labels in
   place (`OverlayViewModel.LoadSession`). A layout switch is structurally the same
   operation — regenerate the sessions with new params, reuse `LoadSession`.
2. **OEM-key-as-command convention.** `;` (`VK_OEM_1`) is currently unused and slots
   next to backtick (`VK_OEM_3`, dim) and backslash (`VK_OEM_5`, suspend). It can
   never be a hint char (input accepts only `A–Z` / `D0–D9`), and any Ctrl chord is
   gated before the OEM block, so Ctrl+C/V stay safe.
3. **Hot-reload + persistence** via `OverlayActionConfig.EnsureFresh` /
   `WriteSetting` — the same path the Options dialog uses.

Prior art (the commercial tool Mouseless ships grid presets + a `cycle grid action
level` command run while the overlay is up) validated the interaction; its
hierarchical-grid geometry model does not transfer, so hunt-and-peck keeps its own
flat synthetic-grid preset format.

# Locked design (user-confirmed)

- **N user-defined presets** in one `GridLayouts` config string; `;` cycles and wraps.
  Ships with 2 defaults (dense + uniform).
- **Persist across restart**: write `ActiveLayout` (index) to `hap.exe.config` on
  each `;`.
- **Grid-only.** Automation and the no-`GridLayouts` legacy path are untouched — `;`
  passes through, geometry from the flat keys. Zero regression.

# Approach (as implemented)

1. **New `Services/GridLayoutConfig.cs`** — `GridLayout` model (5 fields) + pure
   `ParseGridLayouts` (layouts split on `||`, fields on `|`, regions field rejoined
   so commas/stray pipes survive), `ClampActiveLayout` (pure), `ReadGridLayouts` /
   `ReadActiveLayout` / `WriteActiveLayout` wrappers, and `GridLayout.FromFlatConfig`
   (reads the 5 legacy keys — the backward-compat source).
2. **`User32.VK_OEM_1 = 0xBA`** added.
3. **`UiAutomationHintProviderService`** — `EnumGridHints(hWnd, bounds, layout)`
   where `layout == null` ⇒ `FromFlatConfig()` (today's behavior); new
   `EnumGridHintsForBounds(hWnd, bounds, layout)` and `EnumHints(hWnd, layout)`
   overloads added to the service and `IHintProviderService`. `GenerateGridPoints`
   unchanged.
4. **`OverlayViewModel`** — new full ctor carrying `layouts` / `activeLayout` /
   `rebuildSessions`; `LayoutCycleCapable` (computed), `LayoutLabel` ("L2/2"),
   `LayoutBadgeVisibility`, and `CycleLayout()` (advance+persist+rebuild+`LoadSession`
   + pan reset, monitor index clamped). Mirrors `CycleMonitor`.
5. **`OverlayKeyboardHook`** — `OverlayKeyActionKind.CycleLayout`; `Classify` gains a
   `layoutCycle` param (default false); `VK_OEM_1` branch in the `!(ctrl||win)` block;
   hook reads `_vm.LayoutCycleCapable`; dispatch → `_vm.CycleLayout()`.
6. **`ShellViewModel`** — reads `GridLayouts`/`ActiveLayout`; threads the active
   preset into `BuildMonitorSessions` and the single-session path; builds a rebuild
   delegate (`BuildMonitorSessionList` for multi-monitor, `SingleSessionWithTaskbar`
   for Grid+Window) passed into the VM.
7. **`App.config`** — `GridLayouts` (2 defaults) + `ActiveLayout=0` + comments.
8. **Options** — `GridLayouts` multiline editor (Overlay tab); the 5 flat knobs stay
   for the fallback.
9. **Badge** — `LayoutLabel` TextBlock, `Collapsed` when not capable.
10. **csproj × 2** — `<Compile Include>` for the two new files.

# Files touched

- New: `src/HuntAndPeck/Services/GridLayoutConfig.cs`;
  `src/HuntAndPeck.Tests/Services/GridLayoutConfigTest.cs`.
- `src/NativeMethods/User32.cs`, `IHintProviderService.cs`,
  `UiAutomationHintProviderService.cs`, `OverlayViewModel.cs`, `ShellViewModel.cs`,
  `OverlayKeyboardHook.cs`, `App.config`, `OptionsViewModel.cs`, `OptionsView.xaml`,
  `OverlayView.xaml`, `HuntAndPeck.csproj`, `HuntAndPeck.Tests.csproj`, `CLAUDE.md`.

# Verification

- **CI** (`.github/workflows/build.yml`, `windows-latest`): MSBuild compiles + the
  xUnit suite is green (existing + `GridLayoutConfigTest` + `;`-classification cases).
  This is the only local (Linux) gate; UI/P-Invoke is manual on Windows.
- **Manual (Grid + Screen)**: badge `L1/2` on open; `;` → `L2/2` uniform grid; `;`
  wraps; Esc+reopen keeps it; restart keeps it; Automation passes `;` through;
  removing `GridLayouts` reverts to the flat knobs with `;` passthrough.
- Hot-reload: edit `GridLayouts` in Options, re-trigger — applies without restart.

# Risks / notes

- Backward compat is hard-required: with no `GridLayouts`, every path behaves as
  today (`layout == null` → `FromFlatConfig`; `;` → passthrough). Enforced by the
  `layoutCycle=false` default and `LayoutCycleCapable` gate.
- One `hap.exe.config` rewrite per `;` (low frequency; `EnsureFresh` is stat-based).
- Monitor index is clamped, not reset, across a switch (stay on the viewed monitor).
- `Shift+;` (`:`) also cycles (OEM commands ignore shift, matching backtick/backslash).
- Rebuild runs on the UI thread (Grid gen is pure math; Automation is excluded).
